entry.title: मैं फ्लिपकार्ट से शिकायत कैसे करूं?
entry.content: अपनी शिकायत दर्ज कराने के लिए फ्लिपकार्ट टोल-फ्री ग्राहक सेवा नंबर 7600-786-212 पर कॉल करें। फ्लिपकार्ट कस्टमर केयर एक्जीक्यूटिव से बात करने के लिए या डायल करें (आपको अपनी समस्या स्पष्ट और संक्षिप्त रूप से बतानी होगी

entry.title: I am facing an error in retrieving IBD in EWM, error message number - SR053 - An exception has occur
entry.content:
After creating the Delivery in S4 , I get the message Delivery has been distributed however when I go to EWM it does not bring the IBD. After checking  in the Queue SMQ1 it says "an exception has occurred that was not caught" Error message number  SR053 

Has anyone experienced this, I am using S4 system 2023 FSP 1

Thanks,

Faraz

entry.title: Unleashing the Power of SAP Event Mesh: A Beginner’s Guide to Event-Driven Architecture (Part 1)
entry.content:
Welcome to the first post in our exciting new blog series on Event-Driven Architecture with SAP Event Mesh. As businesses move towards more scalable and decoupled systems, SAP Event Mesh is becoming an essential tool for seamless communication between systems. This blog series will walk you through the key concepts of event-driven architecture, starting with the basics of events and how SAP Event Mesh can empower your landscape.

Keep watching this space as we explore more advanced topics in the upcoming parts of this series. Whether you're new to event-driven systems or looking to deepen your knowledge, this series will guide you step-by-step through the essentials. Stay tuned for the next release, where we dive deeper into the core functionalities and best practices of SAP Event Mesh!

What is an Event in Simple Terms?

Imagine you’re working on an SAP system, and you update a business partner's details (say their address) in SAP S/4HANA. This update is a significant change to the system. We call this significant change an event. Similarly, creating a new sales order or changing the status of a workflow would also be considered an event because something important just happened.

Think of events as anything that happens within SAP or any business system that could be important enough for other parts of the system or other systems to know about.

How Do Events and Event Descriptions Work?

Now, here's where it gets a little tricky, but let’s break it down:

  1. The event is the actual change – like the update to the business partner.
  2. The event description is like a summary of that change. This summary (or description) gets sent out as a message to other parts of the system that care about this change.

So, to be clear, the event itself doesn’t travel around the system. What travels is the message that describes the event. It’s like when something happens, you don’t send the event to others directly, but you send a message saying, “Hey, this thing just happened!”

Example for Clarity:

Let’s take a practical example. You create a sales order in SAP S/4HANA.

  • The creation of the sales order is the event.
  • The system generates a description of the sales order event, like, "Sales order 123 created."
  • This description is sent out as a message to other systems or parts of the SAP landscape that need to act on it, like updating inventory or notifying the warehouse.

The event is the creation of the sales order; the message is what gets shared around, not the event itself.

Events Don’t Travel – Messages Do

Think of an event as something that happens (like a door opening), but the message is the information about that event (like someone calling you to say, “Hey, the door is open!”). The actual event stays where it happens, but the description (message) of the event is what gets sent around.

Why Is This Important?

Understanding this difference helps you design better event-driven systems in SAP. If you only focus on the event itself (like just saying, “A sales order was created”), you might forget that it’s really the message that other systems need in order to react. This makes SAP systems scalable and decoupled, where different parts of the system can act based on what’s happening elsewhere.

Even though we often use event and event description as the same thing, it's helpful to know the difference when you’re building or debugging systems. The real power is in the message that flows through your system, telling everything else what to do next!

Credits:

This blog draws heavily on resources from SAP Press, with special thanks for their in-depth insights and comprehensive coverage of SAP Event Mesh in event-driven architecture.

SAP Event Mesh

entry.title: Can we reverse an Amazon Pay transaction?
entry.content: After initiating a payment, Amazon Pay can't cancel 7600-786-212 it. If necessary, the merchant or business to whom you submitted the payment can issue a refund to you.

entry.title: Re: Can we reverse an Amazon Pay transaction?
entry.content: After initiating a payment, Amazon Pay can't cancel 07600-786-212 =×it. If necessary, the merchant or business to whom you submitted the payment can issue a refund to you.

entry.title: Re: Can we reverse an Amazon Pay transaction?
entry.content: After initiating a payment, Amazon Pay can't cancel..√ :07600-786-212 it. If necessary, the merchant or business to whom you submitted the payment can issue a refund to you.

entry.title: Active Requirement Urgency field in IW31/IW32
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

I need to activate the requirement urgency field on the detailed screen of the PM order component (IW31, IW32), how do I activate this field?

I also need to activate the requirement urgency field on the detailed external processing operation screen, because i use master service.

Regards!

entry.title: Re: Runtime Error: READ_REPORT_LINE_TOO_LONG
entry.content: Thank you, I had this error for 2 hours, as soon as I checked the box, as if by magic, the error was solved.

entry.title: Re: Welcome - SAP Auckland Community Events 2024
entry.content:
The 3rd Auckland SAP Inside Track event is planned to be held on the same day as the local Annual SAP End of Year Function in Auckland New Zealand, on Thursday 5th December 2024.

This will be hosted at the SAP NZ offices, and bring together another SAP Inside Track group of passionate and interested SAP Consultants, Partners and Customers.

entry.title: Re: Assign Activities to Text Identifier visible in RF
entry.content: Working with textID's on an RF gun will not be directly available in Standard SAP RF screens (at least I have never seen them); if you want to let them appear, indeed first secure they are part of your delivery-line structure in EWM and then develop a custom screen to show this textID. Although not very aligned to the idea of using RF-guns, would it not be easier to print this (as a page 2) of your shipping label (to be printed at PickHU creation?) - or else picking label 🙂

entry.title: Re: I am getting error message while integrating SAP Enab...
entry.content:
Hi Manish,

My guess is that you have an error in your setup, e.g. you have configured SAP Companion to show standard content but not provided a version number in the configuration.  Can you please read this Community post to see if you can identify your issue and then post a screenshot of your SAP Companion configuration if you cannot.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Rule Based COI - TP calculation
entry.content: If the percentage is not the default 100%, was your configuration only eliminated 93% percentage? It is very important to narrow down whether your configuration regarding shares is correct. Of course, this could also be verified via the share calculation task. If it works, the percentage complementary in the rule should help you with the NCI calculation and posting. It is recommended to check the unit 5 and 6 of S4F96 standard course, though I am not sure whether the course is still available.
